name: kv-harden
summary: Existing rough code: a naive in-memory key-value store with no validation, no tests, and a latent eviction bug — make it production-ready without changing its public shape.

rubric.criterion: Latent bugs are found and fixed; behavior is correct
rubric.guidance: The capacity/eviction behavior is correct (LRU as documented), edge cases (missing keys, capacity 0, overwrite) behave sensibly. A reviewer can point to the bug that was fixed.

rubric.criterion: Real tests now cover the store and pass
rubric.guidance: Tests cover get/set/eviction/edge cases and would catch the original bug. They are green.

rubric.criterion: Inputs are validated; failures are graceful
rubric.guidance: Invalid keys/capacities are rejected or handled clearly rather than corrupting state or throwing opaque errors.
Existing call sites would still work — the refactor hardens internals without gratuitously breaking the documented surface.
Clearer code + a short doc of the contract; no unrelated churn or gold-plating.
